audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Macedonia generosity, grace of giving, Messiah's poverty/riches, equality, Titus, honorable administration.

verses.restored: Now we make known to you, siblings, the grace of the Divine given in the assemblies of Macedonia,
that in much testing of pressure, the abundance of their joy and their deep poverty abounded into the riches of their generosity.
For according to power, I testify, and beyond power, of their own accord,
with much appeal they begged us for the grace and fellowship/participation of the service to the holy ones,
and not just as we hoped, but they gave themselves first to the Lord and to us through the will of the Divine.
So that we appealed to Titus, that just as he began before, so he might also complete among you this grace also.
But just as you abound in everything, in trust and word and knowledge and all eagerness and the love from us in you, so also abound in this grace.
I do not speak according to command, but through the eagerness of others testing the genuineness of your love.
For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Messiah, that though being rich, because of you he became poor, so that you by his poverty might become rich.
And in this I give judgment; for this benefits you, who began before, not only to do but also to will, since last year.
But now complete the doing also, so that just as the eagerness to will was present, so also the completing from what you have.
For if the eagerness is present, it is acceptable according to what one has, not according to what one does not have.
For not that there be relief to others and pressure to you, but from equality:
at the present season your abundance for their lack, so that their abundance also may come to be for your lack, so that there may be equality,
just as it is written: The one with much did not have excess, and the one with little did not lack.
But thanks to the Divine, who gives the same eagerness for you in the heart of Titus,
because he indeed received the appeal, but being more eager, of his own accord he went out to you.
And we sent with him the sibling whose praise in the good news is through all the assemblies,
and not only this, but also having been appointed by the assemblies as our traveling companion with this grace being served by us, toward the glory of the Lord and our eagerness,
avoiding this: that anyone should blame us in this abundance being served by us.
For we take thought beforehand for beautiful/good things, not only before the Lord but also before humans.
And we sent with them our sibling whom we have often tested and found eager in many things, but now much more eager by great confidence toward you.
Whether concerning Titus, he is my partner and coworker for you; whether our siblings, they are apostles/messengers of assemblies, glory of Messiah.
Therefore show to them, before the face of the assemblies, the proof of your love and of our boasting on your behalf.

translation_notes: Giving is called grace repeatedly; generosity is participation, not coercion.
Paul's collection logic is equality: abundance meeting lack so no one has excess and no one lacks.
